rule.name: Project Environment Context
rule.description: The primary development environment is Termux on Android; the primary agent is Gemini CLI. However, the system MUST support multi-context logging from other environments (e.g., CI, local desktop).

rule.name: Prefer POSIX Shells
rule.description: AI agents MUST prefer POSIX-compliant shells (bash/zsh/sh) for command execution. Avoid platform-specific shells like PowerShell unless strictly required.

rule.name: Prefer Quiet Flags
rule.description: Always use quiet or silent flags (e.g., --silent, --quiet, --no-pager) to minimize output while capturing necessary data.

rule.name: Disable Pagination
rule.description: Explicitly disable terminal pagination (e.g., PAGER=cat or --no-pager) to ensure commands terminate and output is fully captured.

rule.name: Capture All Output
rule.description: AI agents MUST capture both stdout and stderr for all shell executions to ensure complete visibility into command outcomes.

rule.name: Exit Code Validation
rule.description: Every shell command execution MUST be followed by an exit code check (non-zero indicates failure).

rule.name: Scrub Secrets
rule.description: AI agents MUST NOT log, print, or commit secrets, API keys, or sensitive credentials. All logged shell data MUST be sanitized.

rule.name: Uniform Log Schema
rule.description: All agents, regardless of context, MUST use a uniform JSON schema for shell-errors.log. Required fields: timestamp, agent_type, agent_session_id, command, exit_code, stderr, stdout, cwd, os_info, shell_info, context.

rule.name: Runtime Context Attribution
rule.description: Log entries MUST prioritize explicit runtime metadata (e.g., agent_type='gemini-cli', os_info='Android/Termux') to enable filtering and context-specific rule generation.

rule.name: Real-Time Error Logging
rule.description: AI agents MUST immediately log any shell command failure (non-zero exit code) to .specfarm/shell-errors.log using atomic appends to prevent log corruption in multi-agent sessions.

rule.name: Context-Aware Error Learning
rule.description: During pre-commit, the system MUST analyze .specfarm/shell-errors.log and convert frequent error patterns into new drift rules. This ensures that a Termux-specific failure does not result in a rule that erroneously blocks a Windows/CI environment.

rule.name: Pre-Commit Drift Gate
rule.description: A pre-commit hook MUST execute drift checks, process shell error logs, update rules.xml, and block commits if unjustified drift is detected.

rule.name: Prefer Standard Utilities
rule.description: Prefer standard Unix utilities (grep, awk, sed, find) over custom scripts or heavy external dependencies in early phases.

rule.name: Prefer Non-Interactive Commands
rule.description: Prefer non-interactive commands. Use flags like --yes, --force, or -y to bypass interactive prompts.

rule.name: Avoid Repeated 'ls /*' Failure
rule.description: Avoid repeated failure of 'ls /*'. Last error (OS: Linux 6.1.145-android14-11-gc1de4747ac59-ab14219743 (aarch64), Agent: gemini-cli): ls: /non-existent: No such file or directory

rule.name: WSL Path Resolution: Backslash vs Forward Slash
rule.description: WSL file tools may fail when passed paths with backslashes (\\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu\...) from Windows context. Error: "Invalid input path" when calling tools like create_file with UNC paths. Solution: normalize UNC paths to Unix format (/home/brett/...) before passing to POSIX tools. Agent: GitHub Copilot, Session: 2026-03-15, Context: paratasks.md file creation.

rule.name: Terminal Interrupt (Ctrl+C) in find Command
rule.description: find command interrupted with Ctrl+C. Error: "^C" in output. Root cause: find was searching a large directory tree (/home/brett/projects/specfarm with 5000+ files). Solution: Add maxdepth limit or timeout to find commands. Agent: GitHub Copilot, Session: 2026-03-15.

rule.name: Executables in bin/
rule.description: All executable scripts (.sh, .ps1) MUST reside in bin/. Executables must have executable permissions set.

rule.name: Logic in src/
rule.description: Core logic modules MUST reside in src/ directory. Entry points and CLI dispatchers belong in bin/ only. Prevents mixing of concerns.

rule.name: XML Validation Required
rule.description: rules.xml MUST validate against rules-schema.xsd on every export and pre-commit. Enforces master rules.xml integrity as single source of truth.

rule.name: Dependency Whitelist
rule.description: Only approved stack items for Phase 2 are allowed: bash, powershell, xml, markdown, git, speckit. External dependencies require explicit approval. Warns on new imports; does NOT block commits.

rule.name: Tests Reference rules.xml
rule.description: Tests MUST reference rules.xml as the master configuration source. Deprecated: direct constitution.md references. Keeps test suites aligned with actual rule enforcement.

rule.name: Rule Phase Tag Required
rule.description: All new rules MUST include a phase attribute (current phase = 2). Legacy rules may be backfilled via manual triage. Enables scoped audits and phase-based rule filtering.

rule.name: Vibe Consistency
rule.description: Rules tagged as interactive (type='warn', type='suggest') SHOULD include a vibe attribute (farm|jungle|plain) for personality consistency. Non-blocking during Phase 2; Phase 3 will enforce.

rule.name: Pre-Commit Drift Blocks
rule.description: Pre-commit hook runs specfarm drift checks and blocks unjustified commits in strict mode. Warnings only in loose mode (default Phase 2).

rule.name: Source Modules Must Be Non-Empty
rule.description: All .sh files in src/ MUST be >= 50 lines. Prevents accidental truncation or removal of core logic during refactors. Exception: config stubs, templates, and explicitly marked stub files can be < 50 lines if documented with a "stub" marker comment.

rule.name: Exported Functions Must Exist
rule.description: All functions declared/called in bin/ entry points (e.g., run_drift_check, dispatch_nudge, export_markdown) MUST exist in their sourced src/ modules. Validated by tests/e2e/test_function_exports.sh during pre-commit.

rule.name: Import Validation in Entry Points
rule.description: All bin/ and src/ entry points that source modules MUST validate sourced modules before use. Post-source validation pattern: if ! declare -f func_name; then exit 1; fi. Prevents silent import failures.

rule.name: Test Coverage for Source Modules
rule.description: Each src/ module MUST have at least one test that validates: (1) file is sourced without errors, (2) all exported functions are present (declare -f check), (3) basic function behavior tested. Improves coverage beyond mere file existence checks.

rule.name: No Partial Refactors in Pre-Commit
rule.description: Pre-commit hook MUST prevent commits with incomplete refactors: functions removed but files kept, modules created but not imported, or import statements without target functions. Validates call sites match definitions using: grep -l "function_name" src/*.sh bin/*.sh

rule.name: Parity Specification Must Exist
rule.description: The orchestrated testing parity specification MUST be present at tests/crossplatform/PARITY-SPEC.md. Documents what "identical output" means across bash and PowerShell for each feature category.

rule.name: Acceptable Differences Must Be Documented
rule.description: tests/crossplatform/acceptable-diffs.md MUST exist and enumerate all known platform-specific output differences (CRLF/LF, path separators, ANSI codes, timestamps) with their normalization rules.

rule.name: Parity Validator Script Must Exist
rule.description: tests/crossplatform/parity-validator.sh MUST exist and implement the normalization pipeline (strip ANSI, normalize paths, normalize line endings, replace timestamps). Enable after T011a is complete.

rule.name: Dual Test Runner Must Exist
rule.description: tests/crossplatform/test-runner-dual.sh MUST exist and run identical test scenarios on bash and PowerShell environments, comparing normalized outputs for parity. Enable after T011b is complete.

rule.name: Cross-Platform Abstraction Layer Must Exist
rule.description: src/crossplatform/platform-check.sh MUST exist for OS detection (Linux/macOS/Windows) and PowerShell version checking. Required before any platform-conditional logic in bin/. Enable after T005–T007 complete.

rule.name: PowerShell Wrapper Scripts Must Exist
rule.description: bin/drift-engine.ps1 MUST exist as a PowerShell-native wrapper that delegates to bash core or provides PS-native parity logic. Required for Windows enterprise adoption. Enable after T009–T010 complete and Windows CI is green.

rule.name: Zero-Dependency Testing
rule.description: All tests MUST be plain bash with no external test frameworks (no pytest/BATS/Jest). Constitutional requirement II.A: zero dependencies beyond bash, grep, sed, awk, git.

rule.name: Task-Context Mode Flag Parsing
rule.description: Task-context mode must parse --task-context flags correctly and generate context-aware rule suggestions from task description, keywords, git history, and constitution references.

rule.name: Confidence Scoring Algorithm
rule.description: Confidence scores must be calculated from weighted components: git history (20%), test links (25%), constitution references (25%), keyword density (30%). Total confidence range 0-100.

rule.name: Line Ending Normalization
rule.description: Line endings must be normalized (CRLF ↔ LF) for cross-platform compatibility in .specfarm/ files (rules.xml, justifications.log, shell-errors.log). Use .specfarm/src/crossplatform/line-endings.sh.

rule.name: Path Normalization
rule.description: Paths must be normalized between Windows (\) and Unix (/) formats via .specfarm/src/crossplatform/path-normalize.sh. Enable on Windows CI runner (test matrix: windows-latest, PowerShell 5.1 + 7.x).
